plan_metadata.name: Teams Agent With Data Custom API Azure OpenAI py Local Debug

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option from the dropdown menu in the "New Project" window.
Click the "General Teams Agent" dropdown option in the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" menu.
Click the "custom API" option from the dropdown menu under "Select an option to load your data" in the "Teams Agent with Data" window within Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" text field within the pop-up window at the top of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.

Type URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/SLdragon/example-openapi-spec/main/real-no-auth.yaml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the VS Code pop-up prompt. Press 'Enter' to confirm.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the OpenAPI Description Document URL input in the URL text field of the VS Code interface.
Click the "Select" dropdown field labeled with "GET/POST methods with at most 5 required parameter and API key" in the pop-up to open the options menu.
Click the "Close" button (red 'x') in the dropdown list titled "Select Operation(s) Teams Can Interact with" at position (795, 46).
Click the "python (preview)" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within Visual Studio Code while setting up the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the directory for the project location (`/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
